accept: Close (and create/claim) error envelope includes the hook's StderrTail when HookFailedError is returned. Format suggestion: 'hook exited N: <last lines of stderr>'.
JSON form preserves the full StderrTail in details.hook_stderr (not just the brief Error() rendering).
Human rendering shows the last ~10 lines of stderr inline so the user doesn't have to re-run the hook to diagnose.
Tests cover both the human and JSON shapes.
payload.description: When .act/hooks/close exits non-zero, CloseErrorOutput.Message is set to err.Error() where err is *hooks.HookFailedError. HookFailedError.Error() renders 'hook exited N (truncated=B)' — the captured StderrTail (last 4096 bytes of the hook's stderr) is dropped. Users see no actionable signal about WHAT failed (gofmt drift, test failure, vet warning, etc.). Verified during act-8277 verification: introduced gofmt drift, ran act close, output was 'hook exited 1 (truncated=false)' and exit=1 — correct gate behavior but the user has to manually run the hook to see what tripped it. Mirror WriteOpAndAutoCommit's hook error path (internal/cli/util.go) — same gap on the create/claim sides.
payload.title: hook failure UX: CloseErrorOutput.Message doesn't surface hook's StderrTail
